course_description:
This course provides a practical introduction to emerging music technologies and their use by musicians and music educators across a range of contexts, including the classroom and studio. The focus of this course is the examination of emerging approaches to the use of music technology and the completion of creative music technology projects related to audio production, sound creation and performance. Students will develop skills for negotiating new apps and technologies. Students will also learn how to record, mix, and produce audio and MIDI through a series of practical and creative production tasks. By the end of the course, students will have developed a range of creative and technical skills for creating and performing music, which are applicable across professional and classroom contexts. Please note that this course offering may be cancelled if fewer than 10 students enroll.

This course provides a practical introduction into emerging music technologies and their use by musicians and music educators, across a range of contexts, including the classroom and studio. The focus of this course is the examination of emerging approaches to the use of music technology and the completion of creative music technology projects related to the audio production, sound creation and performance. Students will learn how to compose and perform using software and apps.ﾠStudents will also learn how to record, mix and produce audio and MIDI through a series of practical and creative production tasks. By the end of the course students will have developed a range of creative and technical skills for creating and performing music which is applicable across professional and classroom contexts.

course_aims:
Investigate a range of music creation apps and software through study and practical activities.

Develop music creation skills within technologically enhanced contexts.

Learnﾠto record, mix and produce audio and MIDI.

Understand the ways music technology is employed across professional and classroom contexts.

Reflect on course experience through scholarly frameworks.

learning_outcomes.description: Successfully use a range of music creation apps and software.
Collaborate with others within contexts which involve music technology.
Record, mix and produce audio and MIDI using professional audio software and equipment.
reflect critically on practice, analyse and evaluate experiences and actions.
Understand the ways music technology is employed across professional and classroom contexts.

assessment_details.title: Practical Projects
assessment_details.due_date: PP1 - Recording in Audacity 18/08/2025 - 22/08/2025 PP2 - Arranging MIDI in Live 8/09/2025 - 12/09/2025 PP3 - Performing with GarageBand 6/10/2025 - 10/10/2025 See Blackboard for the most current information on the assessment schedule.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Complete three in-class tasks on music technology skills. Tasks are collaborative and practical, each focusing on particular technologies and skills introduced during the course. For each task, follow the brief received at the beginning of the lab session, and complete the task by the end of the lab session. * PP1 Recording Audio in Audacity * PP2 Arranging MIDI in Ableton * PP3 Performing with GarageBand This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
According to task instructions on Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
If students are unable attend due to illness or other exceptional circumstance, they may apply for an extension. If approved, students may liaise with the course co-ordinator to negotiate an alternative date to complete the assessment. The alternative date must be suitable for the teaching staff. Students are responsible for following up to ensure suitable arrangements are made to complete the assessment.
Your extension application must be submitted on time, with appropriate evidence.
While your extension request is being considered, you should work towards completing and submitting your assessment as soon as possible.
A late penalty will be applied if you don't submit your assessment on time or if you don't have a valid reason for an extension.
Late submission
You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .
This assessment is completed in-class according to a set schedule. Non-submission of a project without an approved extension results in a mark of 0 for that project.

assessment_details.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .

assessment_details.title: Peer Workshops
assessment_details.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Practical/ Demonstration, Presentation
assessment_details.due_date: Workshops will be held in timetabled Lab classes during Weeks 11, 12, and 13. Students will form pairs and self-allocate a workshop date and topic during Week 5 via an online sign-up sheet . From Week 6 onward, the allocated date is firm and can only be changed via an official extension request.
assessment_details.task_description: Facilitate a Workshop In a group of two, facilitate a 30-minute workshop during your scheduled lab class, introducing peers to a particular technology, app, or software. Timeline In Week 5: Self-allocate a workshop date and topic via the sign-up sheet. Before your allocated workshop date: Conduct independent research to thoroughly learn and understand the allocated technology. Plan an engaging 30-minute workshop session, including demonstrations, explanations, and interactivity. On your allocated workshop date: With your partner, facilitate a 30-minute workshop on your allocated topic. Take charge of timing, classroom engagement, and technology management. Submit your workshop plan to Blackboard. During Weeks 11, 12, and 13: Participate in classmates' workshops and provide feedback on each workshop via a questionnaire. The feedback from the questionnaires will form qualitative data for use in the final essay. Topics The following workshop topics will be available for allocation: Week 11 Peer Workshops - iPad Apps * Koala Sampler * ThumbJam * Loopy HD Week 12 Peer Workshops - Ableton Push Functions * Ableton Push (playing instruments) * Ableton Push (playing drums) * Ableton Push (session control) Week 13 Peer Workshops - Misc. Demonstrations * Korg Volca * Synth * Audacity * MuseScore * Zoom H4n * Free choice (consult with Tutor) Marking Criteria * Operation 30% * Technical Understanding 30% * Communication 20% * Quality of Feedback (on others’ workshops) 20% This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit your workshop plan (maximum 5 pages) to blackboard on your workshop date.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
If students are unable attend due to illness or other exceptional circumstance, they may apply for an extension. If approved, students may liaise with the course co-ordinator to negotiate an alternative date to complete the assessment. The alternative date must be suitable for the teaching staff. Students are responsible for following up to ensure suitable arrangements are made to complete the assessment.
Your extension application must be submitted on time, with appropriate evidence.
While your extension request is being considered, you should work towards completing and submitting your assessment as soon as possible.
A late penalty will be applied if you don't submit your assessment on time or if you don't have a valid reason for an extension.
Late submission
You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .
This assessment is completed in-class according to a set schedule. Failing to deliver the scheduled workshop without an approved extension results in a mark of 0 for all criteria except "Quality of Feedback"

assessment_details.title: Reflective Essay
assessment_details.task_description: Students will write a 2000-word essay on the following question: Reflecting on your allocated technology from the Peer Workshops, consider how this technology contributes to meaningful musical experiences. Draw upon your own experience using the technology, the qualitative data obtained through Peer Workshop Questionnaires, and wider scholarly research. Discuss both the strengths and limitations of the technology. Your essay must: Be within 10% of the word count (1800-2200 words). Draw upon at least 5 scholarly references. Draw upon data collected from your workshop questionnaire. Draw upon your own experience. Adhere to reflective essay style (formal academic language/introduction-body-conclusion structure/use of "I" is permitted where appropriate). Conform to the UQ School of Music Style Guide for referencing and formatting. Be saved as .doc or .pdf for submission (no .pages please). Submit the essay as a via the submission link on Blackboard. Marking Criteria * Understanding 35% * Research 35% * Communication 30% Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit PDF or Word Document to Blackboard Submission Link.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Your extension application must be submitted on time, with appropriate evidence.
While your extension request is being considered, you should work towards completing and submitting your assessment as soon as possible.
A late penalty will be applied if you don't submit your assessment on time or if you don't have a valid reason for an extension.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

learning_resources.other_course_materials: If we've listed something under further requirement, you'll need to provide your own. Required Item Description Further Requirement Portable USB Storage Essential for use in this course and must be brought to every class. At minimum, a reliable 16GB USB stick may be sufficient, however we recommend a good quality portable hard drive with 500GB capacity or more to ensure space for all of your project work throughout the semester. own item needed Studio Monitoring Headphones Compatible with 1/4" (6.5mm) headphone socket are required to complete individual study and assessment items. own item needed

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: Lecture Weekly lectures provide important concepts and contexts for lab and tutorial learning. Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: Laboratory Weekly classes in the Computer Lab involve practical development of skills, collaboration, and in-class assessments. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: Research Tutorial Students will attend a weekly 1-hr research skills tutorial, focusing on research skills within the music technology field, and preparing students for the final essay assessment. Learning outcomes: L04, L05
